- rollout_summaries/REDACTED.md (cwd=/Users/tanishqpalandurkar/Projects/better-ao, rollout_path=/Users/tanishqpalandurkar/.codex/sessions/2026/05/19/rollout-2026-05-19T16-46-26-019e3ff3-8dfa-7873-b81f-f81d919ae8ed.jsonl, updated_at=2026-05-19T14:30:55+00:00, thread_id=019e3ff3-8dfa-7873-b81f-f81d919ae8ed, Playwright-verified tab-switch flicker diagnosis that removed both the named `ViewTransition` wrapper and the residual tab color transition)

### keywords

- Playwright, ViewTransition, session-view-tabs, transition-colors, main-topbar.tsx, session-view-tabs.tsx, app.css, -ua-view-transition-group-anim-session-view-tabs, transitionDuration: 0s, flicker

## User preferences

- when the user asks whether a VS Code Tailwind warning can be accessed and then says “fix it” -> reproduce the live IntelliSense diagnostic instead of guessing from the source alone, then patch the exact shared component they pointed at [Task 1]
- when the user asked to make it a rule in `AGENTS.md` to “use the shadcn ui component instead of creating custom components for maintainability and ergonomic purpose.” -> default to installed shadcn/ui primitives and documented composition when a suitable primitive already exists [Task 2]
- when the user corrected the first sidebar pass with “you again went ahead with a custom solution. you could have used the useSidebar hook from the shadcn/ui's sidebar component instead” -> for similar `better-ao` shell work, start from provider-owned sidebar primitives before adding bespoke collapse state or render gating [Task 3]
- when the user added “Using the sidebar primitives would allow us to enjoy the animations/transitions out of the box as well.” -> preserve primitive-driven animation/transition behavior instead of simulating collapse manually [Task 3]
- when the user said “use playwright to observe that youself,” default to direct browser verification for visual-transition bugs instead of code-only reasoning [Task 4]
- when the user said the motion “doesn't feel continous visually” and then asked to “use the same transition that appears after clicking the collapse sidebar” -> optimize for perceived continuity by reusing the existing sidebar-collapse motion rather than layering a new snapshot animation [Task 3][Task 4]

## Reusable knowledge

- In this repo, Tailwind IntelliSense diagnostics can be reproduced outside VS Code by running the installed extension server `~/.vscode/extensions/bradlc.vscode-tailwindcss-0.14.29/dist/tailwindServer.js` and sending LSP `initialize` plus `textDocument/didOpen` messages [Task 1]
- The canonical-class warning on `translate-y-[calc(-50%_-_2px)]` was `suggestCanonicalClasses`, and the exact replacement that cleared the warning was `translate-y-[calc(-50%-2px)]` [Task 1]
- `AGENTS.md` now explicitly says to prefer installed shadcn/ui components and their documented composition over custom components or hand-rolled markup, while keeping custom UI as the fallback only when no suitable shadcn primitive exists [Task 2]
- `src/features/home/orchestrator-home.tsx` is the home-shell entrypoint for `/home`, and the repo already exposes `SidebarProvider`, `Sidebar`, `SidebarInset`, `SidebarTrigger`, and `useSidebar` from `src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx` [Task 3]
- The durable collapse shape here is one shared `SidebarProvider`, `collapsible="offcanvas"` on both sidebars, `SidebarInset` for center content, and a topbar control that reads `state` from `useSidebar()` while delegating the actual toggle to `SidebarTrigger` [Task 3]
- For the right-side worker rail, `desktopOpen` on the shared `Sidebar` primitive is the clean way to keep the rail mounted while letting primitive-owned offcanvas motion control expanded vs collapsed state [Task 3]
- The terminal-view worker rail should only be open when both the current view is terminal and the provider state is expanded; the landed shape was `desktopOpen={props.open && state === 'expanded'}` in `worker-sessions-sidebar.tsx` [Task 3]
- Playwright proved the original flicker came from two layers: the named React tab `ViewTransition` (`-ua-view-transition-group-anim-session-view-tabs`) and the tab button `transition-colors` crossfade, so both had to be removed for the clean result [Task 4]
- After the fix, Playwright showed no named tab view-transition animation and `transitionDuration: 0s` on the tab buttons, which was the decisive runtime proof that the flicker source was gone [Task 4]
- `pnpm lint:ts` was the reliable static check after both the Tailwind fix and the sidebar refactor, and Playwright DOM inspection was the runtime proof path for collapsed vs expanded shared-sidebar state [Task 1][Task 3]

## Failures and how to do differently

- Symptom: a Tailwind IntelliSense fix is guessed from the class string without proving the actual warning. Cause: the live editor diagnostic was not reproduced. Fix: query the local Tailwind language server first, then patch only the class that produced the diagnostic [Task 1]
- Symptom: a direct `tailwindcss compile` experiment fails with `No loadStylesheet function provided to compile`. Cause: CLI compilation was used as a substitute for IntelliSense diagnostics. Fix: use the extension language server instead of the compile path for editor-warning reproduction [Task 1]
- Symptom: a custom local collapse boolean gets introduced even though the repo already has a shared sidebar primitive. Cause: layout state was reimplemented instead of using provider-owned sidebar state. Fix: start with `SidebarProvider`, `SidebarTrigger`, and `useSidebar()`, and only widen to custom shell state if the primitive truly cannot express the behavior [Task 3]
- Symptom: the worker rail only respects terminal-vs-kanban view and ignores the shared collapse button. Cause: the implementation keyed the rail only off `view === 'terminal'` and skipped provider collapse state. Fix: compose both conditions so the rail is open only when terminal view is active and `useSidebar().state === 'expanded'` [Task 3]
